title: [Bug]: Undefined Field containerConfig.Containers.RootlessNetworking at v3.4.7
body:
### Issue Description



https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.4.7/pkg/specgen/namespaces.go#L327
```
	ns, cniNets, err := ParseNetworkNamespace(network, containerConfig.Containers.RootlessNetworking == "cni")
```


Line 327 referrers a non-exist field "RootlessNetworking".





### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. import 	"github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/specgen" of version 3.4.7 
2. write some code with specgen package
3. build


### Describe the results you received


Build Failed.

```
# github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/specgen
PROJECT_DIR/vendor/github.com/containers/podman/v3/pkg/specgen/namespaces.go:327:80: containerConfig.Containers.RootlessNetworking undefined (type "github.com/containers/common/pkg/config".ContainersConfig has no field or method RootlessNetworking)
FAIL	MY_PROJECT_NAME [build failed]
```

### Describe the results you expected


No error. build successfully.

### podman info output

```shell
Podman version: 3.4.7
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Can you test this against main branch?

> Can you test this against main branch?

main branch is v4 which is not compatible with podman v3.
it will report API is too new and backend is too old, and I run podman on Ubuntu LTS that podman version was locked on v3.
The internal API and Go packages are not supported for outside use.  Please use the bindings in `pkg/bindings` and the REST API.
